content.text:
Here are some of the **top AI headlines as of Thursday, April 9, 2026** that surfaced in major tech coverage over the last couple of days:

- **Anthropic previews a new AI model, “Mythos,” in a cybersecurity push.** TechCrunch reports Anthropic debuted a preview of its new model as part of a cybersecurity initiative on **April 7, 2026**. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/))
- **Anthropic expands its compute deal with Google and Broadcom.** TechCrunch says the company is increasing infrastructure commitments amid strong demand, also reported on **April 7, 2026**. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/))
- **Uber adopts Amazon’s AI chips.** TechCrunch reported on **April 7, 2026** that Uber is the latest company to move toward Amazon’s AI silicon. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/))
- **Google and Intel deepen their AI infrastructure partnership.** This appeared on TechCrunch’s April 2026 news feed and AI category page within the last hour today. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/))
- **OpenAI reshuffles leadership, giving COO Brad Lightcap a new ‘special projects’ role.** TechCrunch listed this on **April 3, 2026** as a notable OpenAI management change. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/03/...))
- **Anthropic is active on multiple fronts:** private-market momentum, a reported biotech acquisition, and a new PAC were all highlighted by TechCrunch on **April 3, 2026**. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/03/...))

**Sources:** I pulled these from current TechCrunch AI and April 2026 news pages, which were updated today and this week. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/))

If you want, I can also give you a **cleaner “top 10 AI news today” roundup** with **1-line summaries plus publisher names and dates**.
